other_observers: Nick Van Lanen, Paul Oppler; Yes
description: A gull most similar to Ring-billed Gull but obviously smaller-bodied, -headed and -billed. White head was heavily smudged gray, appearing "hooded", but with some patchiness suggesting active molt to alternate plumage; dark eye; yellow bill with hint of a dark smudge near the tip of the lower mandible; pure white underparts and tail. Upperparts gray, close in shade to California Gull, but with prominent white scapular and tertial crescents, and broad white tips to secondaries visible in flight. The pattern of black and white on the primaries is best described by the supporting photographic documentation itself. P1-5 are gray with prominent white tips. P6-8 have small white tips, each with a subterminal black spot and a prominent white spot contiguous with the black, with the white spots forming a "string of pearls" effect. P9 is all black with a small white mirror on the outer web near the tip. P10 is all black with a large white mirror on the outer web near the tip.Behaviors: Loafing on shore and swimming in water, and flying over water in close association with a flock of dozens of Ring-billed Gulls.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: n komar, n komar, n komar, n komar, n komar
